title: Next-Lap Plus Horizon Multi-Task Specialist
summary: A recent lap-level F1 preprint uses a cascaded multi-task design: next-lap pit classification plus laps-until-stop regression.
rationale: A recent lap-level F1 preprint uses a cascaded multi-task design: next-lap pit classification plus laps-until-stop regression. That is a good fit here because the next-lap label is noisy near the pit window edge; auxiliary horizon targets can stabilize ranking without replacing the main tabular model.
implementation_hint: From training labels, derive auxiliary targets such as `pit_within_2`, `pit_within_3`, and `laps_until_next_pit` (censored at finish). Train a compact specialist or separate models, then feed out-of-fold auxiliary predictions into the main model or blend by OOF rank averaging; if probabilities matter, apply sigmoid calibration only to the final OOF blend.
expected_effect: Smoother ordering around borderline rows and better recall on imminent but not perfectly timed stops.
risk: Auxiliary targets must be built fold-locally, with race-end censoring handled consistently; calibrators must never see in-fold predictions.
